Title: Doctor Sleep (The Shining, #2)
Author: Stephen  King
My Review:
Written Review
I am currently on a Stephen King high right now... but I won't be reading SK for a while after this but, wow. I love this. I've now read four Stephen King books, and whoa!

Doctor Sleep was incredible for me. Absolutely incredible. A blast from start to finish. I love this as The Shining sequel, and its own story. This magic system is matured, Stephen King has matured in his writing (I just finished IT, and The Shining for context). OH WOW, does this story GO OFF.

This story is something special. I love how King ties in magic and story and the real world. This isn't a full fantasy with creatures and impossibilities, but also this is supernatural. I loved this story.

This story took The Shining to a whole new level. These characters were impressive and multi-layered and emotionally invested. I was fascinated by these characters. The plot was also EXCITING. I had no idea what was going to happen next and that was exciting.

The magic system, as I mentioned, is just awesome this book. I love how King writes his magic systems. The way the world and the extra-worldly interact is superb.

I can't get enough of this story, truly.

More King?
I will certainly be reading more SK. I look forward to reading things like The Stand, Salem's Lot, The Dark Tower series, Insomnia. I might end up reading A LOT of Stephen King. I am impressed so far.


Review Scores

Emotional Impact 5
A BANGER A SEQUEL TO THE SHINING. All over the place, just amazing. Loved this.

Characters 5
WOW. These CHARACTERS ARE DEEP! Even with The Shining starting this story, just wow. Dan = amazing, Abra = amazing, Rose = amazing!

Plot 5
Wow, wow. Just, wow. Magic, the Shine. Holy cow. Just absolutely awesome!

Prose 5
Stephen King, whoa. I'm constantly impressed by Stephen King and how he writes magic, how he writes dialogue, how he writes action. AHHH IT IS SO GOOD.

Worldbuilding 5
Yep, wow. Amazing. Magic, world, sequel to the Shining. HOLY COW.

Total Score 5/5
GOOD GRIEF STEPHEN KING, GO OFF!


Spoiler Story Breakdown
I loved this book. Truly.

As a Sequel to The Shining
This was amazing a sequel to The Shining. I was happy to be back in this world and as I said, this was an excellently matured story too.

They Call it Steam
Now, Steam and Shine. I think this was okay, I wasn't taken completely out of the story because they called it Steam instead of Shine. Ah, it was good.

Dan
An excellent example of character growth and character exploration.

Abra
AWESOME example of the new pupil who is more powerful, yes a trope, but I loved this addition of Abra to Dan.

Rose
AMAZING ANTAGONIST. Strong, smart, quick.

The True Knot
I loved the history and all the different aspects and talents of each characters. Great!

Dan's Half Sister
This was not important at all, I believe. I don't think this was very impactful for the story and not something that I was looking out for. I did notice the wiping of the mouth, but I think this story would've been fine without this addition.


Summary

Following the events of The Shining, after receiving a settlement from the owners of the Overlook Hotel, Danny Torrance remains psychologically traumatized as his mother Wendy slowly recovers from her injuries. The two are living in Florida, but angry ghosts from the Overlook, including Mrs. Massey, the woman from Room 217, still want to find Danny and eventually consume his phenomenal "shining" power. Dick Hallorann, the Overlook's former chef, teaches Danny to create mental lockboxes to contain the ghosts, including that of former Overlook owner Horace Derwent.

As an adult, Danny (now going by Dan) takes up his father's legacy of anger and alcoholism. Dan spends years drifting across the United States, but he eventually makes his way to New Hampshire and decides to give up drinking. He settles in the small town of Frazier on a psychic hunch, working first for the Frazier municipal department and then at the local hospice, and attending Alcoholics Anonymous meetings. His psychic abilities, long suppressed by his drinking, re-emerge and allow him to provide comfort to dying patients. Aided by a cat, "Azzie", that can sense when someone is about to die, Dan acquires the nickname "Doctor Sleep".
In the meantime, Abra Stone, a baby girl born in 2001, begins to manifest psychic powers of her own when she seemingly predicts the 9/11 attacks. She slowly and unintentionally establishes a telepathic bond with Dan through Tony, Dan's childhood "imaginary friend".

As she grows, the contact becomes more conscious and voluntary, and her shining grows stronger than even his. One night, Abra psychically witnesses the ritual torture and murder of a young boy, Bradley Trevor, by the True Knot, a group of quasi-immortal nomads who possess their own psychic abilities. The True Knot members wander across the United States and periodically feed on "steam", a psychic essence produced when people who possess the shining die in pain. They refer to their victims as Rubes. The True Knot's leader, Rose the Hat, becomes aware of Abra's existence and formulates a plan to kidnap Abra and keep her alive, making her produce a limitless supply of steam.

The True Knot begin to die off from measles contracted from Bradley Trevor; they believe that Abra's steam can cure them. Abra asks for Dan's help, and he reveals his connection with Abra to her father David and their family doctor, John Dalton. Though angry and skeptical, David agrees to go along with Dan's plan to save Abra. With the help of Billy Freeman, a friend of Dan, they foil and kill a raiding party led by Crow Daddy, Rose's second-in-command. However, Dan realizes that Rose will relentlessly hunt Abra for revenge. He visits Abra's great-grandmother Concetta, who is dying of cancer at the Frazier hospice, and telepathically learns from her that he and Abra's mother, Lucy, are half-siblings with the same father: Jack Torrance. As Concetta dies, Dan takes her diseased steam into himself. Meanwhile, dissension among the True Knot, along with Rose's obsession with Abra, leads to the group splitting up, leaving Rose with even fewer followers.

Following another kidnapping attempt that Abra foils with Dan's telepathic help, she baits Rose into confronting her at the location where the Overlook Hotel once stood in the Rocky Mountains in Colorado, now home to a campsite owned by the True Knot. Dan and Billy travel to the site while Abra helps them by using her astral projection. Lying in wait, Dan releases the steam collected from Concetta to the remaining group of True Knot members, killing all of them. He also frees the ghost of Horace Derwent to kill the last member, Silent Sarey, waiting to ambush him and Abra, and the two fight Rose in a long psychic struggle. With help from Billy and the ghost of Dan's father, Jack Torrance, they push Rose off an observation platform so she falls to the ground, breaking her neck and dying. Before leaving the campsite, Dan sees his father wave goodbye, having finally found peace.

In the epilogue, Dan celebrates 15 years of sobriety and attends Abra's 15th birthday party. He tells her about the patterns of alcoholism and violent behavior that run in his family and warns her not to repeat them by starting to drink or submitting to rage. Abra agrees that she will behave, but before they can finish the conversation, Dan is called back to his hospice, where he comforts a dying colleague who had antagonized him in the past.

Title: It

Written Review
Now this is something special. Wow. Stephen King has a masterpiece here. I love this. I'm constantly impressed by Stephen King and how he writes and how his stories play out, and how the intricasy of the "magic" interacts on the world. SO GOOD.

This was a beautiful, grusome, epic, magical, terrible, and awesome. There were scenes in this book that had me smiling from ear to ear, there were scenes in this book that had me literally skipping the pages because it was so horrific and disgusting. This is truly something incredible.

Characters 5

Spoiler Story Breakdown
Let's try to do this absolutely incredible epic summarized.

1957
The story starts with the titular yellow coat and floating boat. It's 1957, Bill stutters, and his little brother George has a boat after a flood and waterproof it and George is scared of an IT. 1985

Oh the ending? I loved it, King sets up what is going to happen and it comes through perfectly. The ending not being great is blown out of proportion, certainly.

Title: Carrie
Author: Stephen  King
My Review:
Written Review
Another boom from Stephen King, and this is his FIRST PUBLISHED NOVEL?! Yes, this was an absolute dream. I must bring to attention that I listened to this novel. That might affect my scores or my review, but I really enjoyed this novel.

I listened to this novel once before, early January last year, and this time was again another wonderful read. I just read The Shining by Stephen King and oh wow, Stephen King is something special.

For most of my reading, lately, I have been reading Fantasy. So, horror is an interesting genre. Stephen King feels like his very own genre. But, I truly enjoy the Stephen King novels I’ve read so far.

Carrie, the novel, is a very interesting design. This story, or reading, is a “recap”, and I feel that doesn’t truly describe this. The situation has occurred and the story we read is telling what happened. A very cool use of plot.

These characters are very well described and designed. Great work again Stephen King. Also, another great example of the supernatural inside of the natural.

Review Scores

Emotional Impact. 4.
I was on the edge of my seat from the first page. This is a story that is of an incident that has already happened, so things are described as “that happened and he died”, so that means that we know the “he” is going to die. But really, this was a good book!


Characters. 5.
Another great Stephen King example of real characters with real motivations.


Plot. 5.
Quick, exciting, smart.


Prose. 5.
Stephen King, real, smart, quick, excellent.


World Building. 4.
What I think about, when it comes to world building, is building the whole world. However, that isn’t always very necessary. This novel is very well described and tells what the world is like, and how things occur. This is a particular incident that is being explained, but still a good example of world building.

Total Scores 4/5
I am a huge fan of Stephen King, only after 2 novels.

Title: The Shining (The Shining, #1)
Author: Stephen  King
My Review:
Written Review
Oh my, my, my! This was an absolute treat to read! This book was scary, and exciting, and really fun to read. There were twists and turns all over the place! This book was released in 1977, so there were a lot of things that could have been spoiled for you if you've been interested in this story at all. So, going into this story had been spoiled for me a bit, with the movie out as well. But, since I've finished the book, the movie actually changed some things. SO, not too much was spoiled for me.
 
With that said, THIS WAS AWESOME! I really enjoyed reading this book. I enjoyed this story, and I was impressed with Stephen King's writing. I'd only ever listened to Carrie by Stephen King before and whoa, THIS WAS AWESOME!

I loved the supernatural elements of this story, and how that was cleverly woven in with the "real world" characters, this was just an interesting story to read throughout. I think it's important to point out that I generally read ONLY FANTASY. So, reading this was a bit of a departure, but something that I have been looking forward to reading for some time now.

I'm actually not going to spoil this story in anyway for you, even though it's been out for so long. I don't think that is important. I think you should go into this story as blind as you can. It is a treat to read.

I wanted to be scared by this book, and I was. But I wasn't so scared that I couldn't read this in the dark where any bump in the night would scare me. But it was scary.

The characters are VERY well developed and very believable here. There are some impressive characters here.

The setting is expertly crafted and really Stephen King does a great job communicating isolation.

Also, a very solid ending.


Review Scores

Emotional Impact. 5.
I WAS HOOKED as soon as this story started. And throughout. This was a great story.

Characters. 5.
Yes please, these characters were absolutely incredibly well written. Solid motivations and very real. Smart, and characters were very well separated from each other in their thoughts and feelings, and communication styles.

Plot. 5.
Something that I like to communicate about this story is "believable", but obviously "the shine" is not real, but I think this was an exciting, scary, and well developed plot!

Prose. 5.
Now this is something that I really enjoy. I am imporessed with Stephen King and how he communicates everything. Beautiful, real, and exciting.

World Building. 5.
This is something that I am again impressed with! I will mention "the shine" and that is all. I think this was well executed and well described. There world was described and there were hints of more to come in the world.

Title: The Lightning Thief (Percy Jackson and the Olympians, #1)
Author: Rick Riordan
My Review:
Started Reading
I have started reading this September 13, 2023! I originally read this book when I was in seventh grade, so I was only 12 or 13 when I first read this. That is CRAZY!

At the time, it was the longest book I'd ever read, at 375 pages! I did, however, read 20 books that year! WOW! Small books, mind you. But, I loved reading books then, and now I love reading again!

Written Review
Well I was pleasantly surprised with this book. I honestly didn’t remember anything that happened in this book, besides the answer to a mystery that was answered near the middle of the book.

I really enjoyed this book! The characters were fun, the integration of the Greek mythology! Obviously, but I found it very easy to understand. If you know the lore and mythology it might help you know what is going to happen before it is going to happen.

The beginning of the book kicked off with a bang, and I think that’s important for this book. It leads to an exciting story. I think the character work here is fine. We truly focus on a few main characters and there are a few side characters. Some are more interesting than others.

The story is part quest fantasy, with Greek mythology built in, as well as a roadtrip story. There are parts that are exciting and suspenseful, but there are also parts that are slow and should’ve been cut. There are also parts that could’ve had more time.

Overall, I really liked this book.

What is The Lightning Thief
The Lightning Thief is a light-hearted fantasy about a modern 12-year-old boy who learns that his true father is a Greek god, I won’t spoil that for you!

Percy sets out to become a hero by undertaking a quest across the United States to find the entrance to the Underworld and stop a war between the gods. The novel provides a high-interest, humorous introduction to the Greek myths.

It works well if taught in conjunction with mythology, which is a core component of most English state curriculum frameworks. The novel can also be taught at any time after the introduction of Greek mythology, to draw on students’ prior experience as per standard three of the Standards for English Language Arts of the National Council of Teachers of English.

Review Scores
Emotional Impact. 3.
I really enjoyed this book, I did. I was invested for the most part, but the story left a little to be desired for me. I like where we’re going at the end.

Characters. 3.
I think these characters were well developed, however, they were a tad one-note. What we learned about them at the beginning of this book - at the end of the book there wasn’t much growth. Could be pretty slight, but I didn’t see much.

Plot. 3.
Now that I sit down and write out my scores, I’m very mid on this book. I did enjoy it, but there were parts in this book that needed to be shortened or lengthened to improve the plot. I understand what the plot was supposed to be, it just felt a little convoluted at points.

Prose. 4.
Nothing too great or too bad here. Easy to read.

World Building. 4.
Again, nothing too great or too bad. I’m excited to see the world expand in future books. We did cross the country here, and that was explored, but we saw very small sections of the world without much outside perspectives.

Total Score 3/5
This is a book that I will reread as I continue the series, but I will not be seeking to reread this book anytime soon.


Spoiler Story Breakdown


The Good
Kronos, I’m excited about this. Kronos was like the big bad that was in the deep dark background for the whole story and it looks like this is building into something very exciting for our characters. I am interested to what happens with the story from here.

Percy, Annabeth, and Grover, the chemistry between these three was great. I really appreciated their teamwork and how they communicated. I think the plot lended to how their powers worked together and how important it was that Percy had companions. GOOD STUFF!


The Bad
Ares, I think the Ares fetch quest for Ares’ shield was odd, almost. Out of place. But I guess it makes sense for Ares’ character, but it just seemed odd. Like something Ares couldn’t do - but I guess Ares had Percy do that so Percy could make a fool of himself for the gods.

Hades, this was an interesting section of the book. We were leading up to this the whole time and then boom - SWITCH! Now that’s not the problem, the problem I had was how easy it was. And again that is just Hades character. He isn’t a BAD guy really, he’s just the Lord of the Dead and has really bad things tied to him. I just think it was very easy for Percy, Annabeth, and Grover just got to leave. Ah, maybe it was alright. Maybe it’s just mid.

Is this book just mid? A YA fantasy novel? I can see that being a real possibility.

Title: Tress of the Emerald Sea
Author: Brandon Sanderson
My Review:
Written Review
Oh wow, I absolutely LOVE THIS BOOK! I am constantly impressed by Brandon Sanderson. If you've never read any of his novels, GO DO IT!

This book is a lovely story of a girl who loses her love, and must go on a huge adventure to go find him. Along the way she will be tried and tested on her abilites. She will learn an incredible about herself.

This story is written from a narrator's perspective, as sometime who is involved in the scene, and makes for a wonderful story with a neat perspective! I don't think I've ever read another story like this. Maybe I have, but this was so much fun. I can't say enough about how fun this story was. Not only from the narrator's perspective; but also the story itself. Just fun and whimsical!

This story is also VERY COSMERE. And that is something I've been excited for, for a while now. The magic system here is brand new, and it is very easy to understand.

Find more in my spoiler review! Go RAFO why I loved this book so much!

What is Tress of the Emerald Sea
Tress of the Emerald Sea is a stand alone cosmere novel, told as an in-world story by Hoid. It chronicles the journey of Tress as she travels the world of Lumar to rescue her beloved, Charlie, after he is kidnapped by the Sorceress. Unlike other Hoid stories which tend towards more fanciful, Tress' adventures reflect real-world cosmere events that occurred, though the tale still includes artistic liberties added by Hoid as part of his storytelling style.

Tress of the Emerald Sea was the first of the secret projects released in 2023. It was released in January of that year as part of a Kickstarter Campaign.[1] This was followed by a standard publishing release on April 4, 2023. It is a cosmere novel set on a new world, with the "craziest worldbuilding" of the four secret projects.

On March 3, 2022, Brandon released the first five chapters along with some brief commentary, which can be read on his website and listened to on his YouTube channel. He also did a full spoiler Q&A about these preview chapters, which can be listened to on his YouTube channel.

Review Scores

Emotional Impact 5
This was so much fun! And that will hold to all of these pillars of the review scale. Emotional Impact, please! I was on the edge of my seat for most of this book. I laughed, and I mean truly, OUT LOUD! SO MUCH FUN!

Characters 5
This was a great character book! I am obsessed with Hoid and Tress and this crew! Oh, but I will say that Charlie left a little bit for me to be desired. Luckily, we didn't have too much of Charlie. This was an incredible character told story, very character focused of course. I can't find the word, but we are CAMPED OUT in the character perspectives. SO GOOD.

Plot 5
UGH, I was obsessed with this story. We have amazing magic, amazing plot. We start small, we open the world up, we have struggles, we have so much fun! Also, COSMERE. Wow, this world is ripe with Cosmere items. SO GOOD.

Prose 5
I could gush about this for HOURS! This book is written in Hoid's voice. Need I say more? I will. Hoid, as Brandon writing Hoid, is taking his time with the introspection here. There is so much in this book to deal with prose. I love it. There are forth wall breaks, can I say that about written word? But wow. I love this.

World Building 5
Cosmere, need I say more?! This is full Cosmere. We get, calling out books and series, things from Elantris, from the Stormlight Archive, from Mistborn, from Warbreaker.. which is Stormlight Archive. SO GOO!


Spoiler Story Breakdown

Summary
The story follows Tress, a girl living on an island in the middle of an ocean of green pollen, one of twelve covering her home planet. A window-washer and an avid collector of cups, Tress is friends with Charlie, who pretends to be the groundskeeper for the duke that rules the island while in actuality being his son. Over time, Tress and Charlie realize they have feelings for each other and confess. Unfortunately, the duke sees this, and shortly after takes Charlie with him to other islands to marry him off to a princess.

Before his departure, Charlie promises to fend off all the suitors and send Tress letters and cups. She receives four of them before Charlie falls silent, so when his ship returns, she ventures out to see what became of him. However, the duke brings back a different heir, while Charlie appears to have disappeared completely. Tress investigates this and discovers that Charlie was sent off to marry the sorceress of the Midnight Sea, and was subsequently captured. Despite the sorceress sending a ransom note, no-one is interested in paying. Realizing that everyone but her has put Charlie out of their minds, Tress decides to set out and rescue him herself.

Please check out the Interior Art page {Tress of the Emerald Sea/Interior art - The Coppermind - 17th Shard}

Hoid
Now, let's talk Hoid.. I am obsessed with Hoid. Now, I love Hoid, or Wit.. lol. But this is a full story told as Hoid being the Narrator. He is hilarious, and intelligent, and this book flips between third person and first person perspective because he is actually in this story. He is cursed during almost the entire story. I will say, HOID MADE THIS STORY, now Tress is awesome and the story is incredible, but Hoid is incredible.

Tress
Tress was an absolutely amazing main character here. She was smart and well established. She was funny. I think she was a STRONG character. I think her whole progression was beautifully crafted. I mean I could just try to explain what a story is with how a character starts with a small world then ends in a full world - but WOW. Tress is so practical, as she said, but she IS. She learns so much this book. She struggles. She needs help. She is great! Does she need Charlie though??

The Crew
Brandon did not need to dig so deep into the backstories of nearly every single one of these characters. Not including the Dougs! lol. We get so much for each character and we learn so much about them. The characters are so good too. I love a good crew story, with people that are important for specific jobs. I really enjoyed this book. I truly really enjoyed what we got from this book. I didn't need more to make this any better. It was good!

Xisis the Dragon
This was absolutely beautiful and fanciful and made me so exciting. The dragon reminded me a lot of Smaug, and makes me want to go back and reread The Hobbit. Oh it was just so much fun! That was also a fun scene. LOVED IT.

Riina the Sorceress
Wow, ahh, please read! You have to read it. I can't say much more because she was an exciting villian here. Also, don't make bets with Hoid lol. Also, she's got a spaceship!

Charlie
So, Charlie. There was a point in this story, once Tress got to the Sorceress' Tower and got this body that wasn't actually Charlie. I believe he smiled and then I thought, OH NO! Tress was making SO MUCH personal progress and then to have him come back... I'm just glad for the resolution we got. Also, Charlie was Huck the rat. That was shocking for me!

The Writing
This is so good. But, this world, and the way Hoid communicates this world, is incredible. There are points that truly STOP the story to breakdown what is going on. There is a character, Fort, who is deaf. But that's not it. He has broken fingers so he wouldn't be too good at sign language, so he has a magical tablet that helps him basically have closed captions for people speaking to him. But the story stops to help us readers who don't know much about a lot of things in the wold - lip reading, that isn't something that all hearing impared people can do. It isn't 100% accurate and more. I didn't describe that very well, but you're going to have to RAFO.

The Cosmere Connections
I almost don't want to spoil this, and I really won't. The Cosmere books that I've read aren't ALL THE BOOKS, but this book includes things from a lot of Cosmere worlds. Previous books have very shallow hints, but now, Brandon has said "the gloves are off", and now YES THE GLOVES ARE OFF!!

Go RAFO why I loved this book so much!

Title: A Memory of Light (The Wheel of Time #14)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Written Review


This has been one of the most impactful books I've ever read. Not just the Wheel of Time, but in the fantasy genre. This is an incredible ending to the Wheel of Time. I couldn't have asked for a better ending. You will be TIRED after reading this book. You will have to grab tissues, and you'll have to make sure you have some hydration as you read. Why? Because when you start reading this book, you won't be able to stop.

This book is action-PACKED! I'd say this book is 95% action. You are on such a high for 95% of this book, the last 5% you have to hold on TIGHT. You might struggle at points, but you ARE IN FOR A TREAT!

I think, and I've said it before, Brandon Sanderson was perfect to complete this series. Now, Brandon has said that Robert Jordan had written the ending to these books YEARS ago. Brandon has also said that RJ had written the epilogue with very little touched by Brandon.

Since Brandon has taken over, the magic system has been more deeply integrated - if that was even possible. Robert Jordan, in his passing, left Brandon such a well developed story - in my eyes. Robert Jordan is a master in story telling. Brandon Sanderson is a master in story telling.

Also, I love Perrin now. He has become one of my favorite characters since The Gathering Storm. If only he had been this incredible in books 5-12.

I can't believe I finished The Wheel of Time... wow.


Review Score


I cannot even describe how much of a 5/5 this book is for me. Oh wow, did I struggle at the beginning of this book, yes. I was struggling with the amount of Trollocs, but.. I'll talk more about that in the Spoiler Story Breakdown.

Emotional Impact, 5
HO-LY SMOKES. Yes, my emotions were thoroughly impacted! I was whooping and hollering constantly while reading this book. The way this story progresses, wow. This was incredible.

Characters, 5
SUCH DEVELOPMENT OVER THE PAST 13 BOOKS! This book does not disappoint. These characters, we love them, we hate them, we want them to succeed. We HURT when they die. We HURT!

Plot, 5
YIKES. YASS. THIS BOOK Y'ALL!

Dialogue / Prose, 5
Beautiful. WELL WRITTEN. Battles well described. It doesn't get better than this.

Cinematography / World Building, 5
Without spoiling this book, you will not be disappointed here. YIKES!


Spoiler Story Breakdown


To do this book justice.. this is going to be difficult.

This book starts on a bang, Trollocs invading Caemlyn from the ending of TOM. And here begins our Trollocs battle. And that won't stop until page 970. Does that sound like a lot? YES. BUT! YOU WILL NOT BE TIRED OF THEM BY THEN. Holy smokes. I was struggling at the beginning of this book because of the Trollocs, because they were EVERYWHERE. You'd go over here and they were there, you'd go over there and they were there.

But we are DEEP in the Last Battle. And GOOD GRACIOUS! The Last Battle in this book is 216 pages, one chapter. An INCREDIBLY EPIC BATTLE, like hard to describe actually. But there are over 50 perspectives.. 50 perspectives. But I'm jumping ahead of myself.

A couple standout characters here that surprised me.. Perrin, Androl, Olver, and Birgitte. Perrin has come up a lot in the last books, as I've said, and he is just AWESOME in this book!

Let's dive in.
Thank you to {Book Summary: A Memory of Light - Dragonmount} for the helpful summaries here.

The war begins on four fronts. Elayne and Bashere lead Andorans against the Trolloc army in Caemlyn. Agelmar and the Borderlander armies support Lan’s forces at Tarwin’s Gap. Gareth Bryne and Egwene face an army in Kandor. Ituralde and the Aiel will lead the forces at Shayol Ghul in defense of Rand. Faile is placed in charge of supplies and entrusted with the Horn of Valere.

At the Black Tower, Androl and Pevara bond one another and lead the infiltration in the Black Tower to save Logain, eventually driving out Taim and the Dreadlords that were being Turned to the Shadow.

Perrin and Gaul have Rand help them travel to Tel’aran’rhiod in the flesh to hunt Slayer. Rand arrives at Shayol Ghul and enters the cave. Perrin and the wolves save him from Slayer. Perrin also discovers Compulsion is being used to manipulate the four great captains commanding the forces of the Light. Egwene and the Aes Sedai army in Kandor are overrun when Demandred arrives with all the forces of Shara. They are eventually reinforced by the arrival of the Seanchan. Similarly, Bashere leads Elayne’s army into a trap outside Cairhien, but they are saved by Logain, Androl, and the Black Tower.

Mat takes control of all the armies of the Light and prepares a final stand at the Fields of Merrilor. But Faile, Olver, and the Horn are lost in the Blight.

Rand duels Moridin, then faces the Dark One with conflicting possible futures outside of the Pattern while the Final Battle rages. During the prolonged battle, Demandred duels Gawyn and kills him, then wounds Galad, while dueling Mat’s army.

Losses in battle also include Rhuarc, Siuan, Bryne, Birgitte, and more. Mat sends the Seanchan away in a feigned retreat. Egwene defeats Taim and the Dreadlords with a new weave, the Flame of Tar Valon, which saves the Pattern from the damage of balefire. Lan duels Demandred and finally kills the Forsaken commander. At the same time, the return of the Seanchan and Olver blowing the Horn of Valere turns the tide and they defeat the army of the Shadow and the Sharans.

Logain breaks the seals and Rand baits Moridin into using Callandor. Rand then uses saidin, saidar, and the True Power to build a new, perfect prison for the Dark One. In the final stage of battle, Perrin protect Rand by killing Slayer and Cyndane. Then Mat arrives and destroys Padan Fain.

Rand is believed to have died of his wounds after the battle and a funeral pyre is lit. Aviendha, Elayne, and Min know he survived in Moridin’s body as he rides away in secret, unable to channel but still affecting the Pattern directly.

My Thoughts


Rand
This was an incredibly flawless ending to this series. Rand going off in the sunset, being able to hide. Rand doing this body-swap with Moridin, because of the balefire crossing in POD, I have been thinking about the balefires crossing since then and it comes to fruition here. Rand also lighting the pipe.. I agree with Brandon Sanderson’s thoughts, that Rand being so intwined with the Pattern - he can just manifest things now. How powerful!

Egwene
I’d been spoiled on Egwene’s death a long time ago. However, her death scene was still so impactful. From TFOH, I’d fallen in love with Egwene. And from TGS she has become just incredible and every scene she’s on the page in itching for more. So her death, while sacrificing herself was perfect.

Nynaeve
To think about Nynaeve, specifically in AMOL, is difficult. But also the question is brought up, what did Moiraine do here besides be there? Nynaeve, we’ve heard is the most powerful female channeler that has been for over 1,000 years. But in this book, she stood next to Rand in Shayol Ghul, then Linked with Rand. Then gave Alanna some herbs as a great Wisdom would do. But what else? Nynaeve was one of my favorites for a VERY LONG TIME! I’m not upset, there was so much more that occurred in this book. Just a thought.

Mat
Son of Battles! Oh man Mat, this was INCREDIBLE! I loved everything Mat here.

Perrin
Oh Perrin, I’ve gushed over you this review and I can’t stop. YOU ARE SO POWERFUL and so AWESOME! I apologize that it took me this long to realize. You’re amazing. TAR is yours. Also, I’m really glad Faile was alive.

Olver
Olver blew the Horn of Valere! That is awesome!

Gawyn
People have blown Gawyn out of proportion, in my humble opinion. He put on the bloodknife rings and ran to kill Damandred. He got himself killed and Egwene was shaken to her core, but that didn't really do much. This wasn't too big of a deal to me.

The New Seal on the Dark One's Prison
Logain breaks the seals and Rand baits Moridin into using Callandor. Rand then uses saidin, saidar, and the True Power to build a new, perfect prison for the Dark One. This was awesome. For the whole battle with Rand and the DO throughout the entirety of this book, I was impressed with this realization. Does this mean the DO is sealed forever.. I think so!

Title: Towers of Midnight (The Wheel of Time, #13)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Written Review


Why yes, I THOROUGHLY ENJOYED THIS ENTRY! This one struggled a bit for me being a middle book on a trilogy. A lot was resolved, but there was a lot left open.. obviously as this penultimate novel in a 15 book series. We are not done yet, and there is still something big to come! And there was even some plots STARTED this book.

A satisfactory entry into the series. This one certainly separates itself as primarily written by Brandon Sanderson. There are friendly cameos from friends of the Wheel of Time community. There are beautiful one-liners. Great action. The dialogue between the characters is just so crisp. Oh this was a great, and I keep saying entry, because it is - it is an entry into the Wheel of Time!

I am ecstatic to start A Memory of Light next week! Book thirteen complete, what's left.. book fourteen and I've left the prequel New Spring for after the series to complete my first read-through of The Wheel of Time!

Spoiler Story Breakdown



Lan has gathered over 12,000 from the Borderlands and has raised his title Lord of the Seven Towers, Defender of the Wall of First Fires, Bearer of the Sword of the Thousand Lakes. We leave Lan charging into battle with over 150,000 Shadowspawn at Tarwain's Gap..

Moiraine oh wow, Moiraine is back! Moiraine was kept in the Aelfinn and Eelfinn world and they fed on her Power and she is weakend, but she has an ivory bracelet angreal. Mat, Thom, and Noal (Jain Farstrider) saved her from the snakes and foxes. Moiraine and Thom professed their love and he mentioned he would be her Warder.

Noal turns out to be Jain Farstrider, and he sacrificed himself to help save Moiraine.

Elayne was able to reconnect with Galad, Gawyn, Mat, Thom, Perrin, and... MORGASE. Elayne has also borrowed the foxhead ter'angreal from Mat and was able to create copies of the ter'angreal in exchange for help with Aludra's dragons. Elayne now has the throne of Cairhien.

Morgase married Tallanvor.

Aviendha goes to Rhuidean and goes through the columns and then goes a second time.. and sees the future of the Aiel. She has a plan to help sto pthat from happening.

Nakomi.. who is this?! She has showed up and disappeared after talking with Aviendha.

Nynaeve OH MY GOSH, HEALS MADNESS and passes her test for the shawl and takes Lan's bond from Myrelle.

Egwene believes Rand is mad, intending to break the Seals and she summons armies wtih the intent to stop him. Egwene also bonds Gawyn as Warder! Egwene faces Mesaana in TAR in the reflection of Tar Valon and Mesaana and the Black Ajah are there. Egwene breaks Mesaana's mind in a battle of wills.

Perrin trains with Hopper in the wolf dream (which I'll come back to this), and finds a strange wall.. turns out to be a dreamspike setup by Slayer/Isam/Lord Luc. Perrin also was judged by Morgase against Galad and the Whitecloaks - they considered Perrin a Darkfriend for killing some Whitecloaks. Morgase declared guilty and gave over the punishment to Galad. Perrin then saved Galad and the Whitecloaks from a Trolloc attack. Perrin and Hopper steal the dreamspike and end up destroying the dreamspike from Slayer. Hopper is killed. Perrin learns not to fear the wolf inside him and reunites with Noam, the wolfbrother. Oh, Perrin had a lot this book. Perrin crafted a Power-Wrought hammer with the help of Neald, the Asha'man, and Wise Ones and now the Asha'man might be able to make more Power-Wrought Weapons - the Aiel really want those! Perrin also made steward of the Two Rivers.

Graendal was the one who sent Slayer and the dreamspike to Perrin, and is now punished by Shaidar Haran.

Olver wins a game of Snakes and Foxes, and now plans on killing the Shaido who killed his father.

Caemlyn is being seiged by Shadowspawn from the Waygate in Caemlyn.

Mat killed the gholam.. still means there might be 5 left right? Mat, Thom, and Noal entered the Tower of Genjei and saved Moiraine. Mat had his eye removed by the Eelfinn to give Moiraine her freedom. Using his ashandarei to open a doorway out of the Tower of Ghenjei.

Rand comes down from Dragonmount, and is changed. His presence makes all things better. He visits Egwene and announces he will break the Seals of the DO at the Field of Merrilor. Rand apologizes to his friends, and goes to restore order in Bandar Eban, he also helps save Bashere and Ituralde. King Alsalam is also found alive. Rand also meets with the Borderland monarchs. He is ready to start the Last Battle.

Wolf Dream vs TAR okay, so these have to be the exact same thing right?! It isn't fully written out on the page, but also Perrin can see Egwene and they can communicate with each other. So this HAS THE BE THE SAME THING! I'm just waiting to see this on the page.

The Forsaken alive.. Damodred, Lanfear / Cyndane, Ishamael / Moridin, Moghedien, Graendal and Sammael (thinking still alive). Dead.. Asmodeon. Balefired.. Rahvin, Semirhage, Balthamel, Be'lal, Aginor.

The Seanchan now have Travelling from Suffa.. Elaida. They plan to use Traveling to attack the White Tower again!

The Black Tower something is happening here. Androl is building a bit of a resistance against Taim. There must be a dreamspike at the Black Tower because no one can make gateways in or out of the Black Tower. HMMM.

Lanfear in the epilogue Rand hears a woman crying and Rand shows up and it's Lanfear.. She is scared. I think Lanfear might be being saved by Rand?!?! Interesting.

Field of Merrilor is the place Rand will meet with monarchs, the Amyrlin Seat Egwene, Perrin and his forces, The Band of the Red Hand, the Whitecloaks, also known as the forces of the Light.



Who Killed Asmodeon so, I watched https://youtu.be/lfOhlxYiKQs?si=6qu-XEt33Q7fP69w&t=2220 Wheel of Time - A Memory of Light reading / q&a session , and you can watch this too. You'll have to look in the glossary... I won't spoil it for you.

Title: The Gathering Storm (The Wheel of Time, #12)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Written Review
Oh yes, thank you Robert Jordan and Brandon Sanderson. If you've read Brandon Sanderson, you'll know how Brandon Sanderson likes to end his book... the Sanderlanch as it is called. And this book is NO EXCEPTION to that. Speicially, I feel like Robert Jordan is the absolute master at backstory, build up, and world building. Brandon Sanderson is the absolute master at action, magic, pacing, and endings. I think The Wheel of Time was perfect for Brandon Sanderson. The Gathering Storm was an incredible starting point for Brandon Sanderson's Wheel of Time novels.

Now, would you have known this book had a different writter than before? Honestly, I don't think so. There was an incredible entry in The Wheel of Time! WOW! So good.

Some chapters were absolutely difficult to read, some made me jump with joy, some made me very emotional. This was a beautifully written and excellent book.


Spoiler Story Breakdown

Holy smokes, so much happened in this book.. oh this book was so good. Wow.

Perrin after saving Faile is moving his army, which now includes a LOT of refugees from Malden. Perrin also tries to work in the Wolf Dream. I'm so interested in the Wolf Dream.


Faile killed Masema.


Mat misses Tuon and ends up in a village of Hinderstap, which has a bit of a strange situation that occurs after sunset - they basically become enraged and kill people and then the next day, it's all good. Verin also shows up to Mat. Verin agrees to take Mat and the Band to Caemlyn.


Aviendha ends up finally realizing that she is ready to be a Wise One. The Wise Ones send Aviendha to Rhuidean to complete her Wise One training.


Gawyn oh Gawyn. Gawyn seperated from his crew who killed Warders and learned about Egwene being captured in the White Tower. Gawyn goes to the Rebel Aes Sedai camp to help save Egwene. To be continued..


Siuan has bonded Gareth Bryne! Also to be continued..


Tuon is now Empress. Tuon is now Fortuona, her new name. She met with Rand and ended up declaring he needs to kneel to her. He doesn't and Fortuona declares the Seanchan are going to attack the White Tower... to be continued..


Now the BIG STUFF

Verin is BLACK AJAH. Yes, wow. Verin commits suicide, which she had to do to tell Egwene what was happening. The Dark One's oaths require, "I swear not to betray the Great Lord, to keep my secrets until the hour of my death." Verin has been an absolute awesome character, for me, since we met her. I was shocked when the reveal occured that she was Black Ajah, but she studied the Black Ajah as a mole. She gave Egwene a lifetime of research to Egwene, including all known members of the Black Ajah. I'm going to miss Verin. She also gives Egwene her blessing for Egwene being Amyrlin. So good.


Egwene is now reached one of my top 5 characters now! OH SO GOOD. Alright so Egwene was being beaten by the Mistress of Novices, Silviana, constantly as she is still telling people that Egwene is the true Amyrlin. Egwene is convincing Novices, Sitters, and as many people as she can to sew deceit toward Elaida. Egwene wants the Tower to be together. She also gets with the Black Ajah hunters and convinces them as well. Egwene also told the rebel Aes Sedai not to rescue her. And then the Seanchan attack the White Tower.

Egwene stands up and unites Novices and Egwene gets a pwerful ter'angreal and absolutely wrecks the Seanchan. Egwene is absolutely super powerful with this ter'angreal, and she gets very, very tired after helping stop the Seanchan. She ends up falling asleep and Gawyn ends up showing up with Siuan and Bryne and takes her away from the Tower.

Egwene, in the rebel Aes Sedai camp, gathers the Hall together and tells them all about the Black Ajah list that Verin gave her, including Sheriam. Egwene and the rebel Aes Sedai are able to still and execute 50 of the 70 Black Ajah sisters, the rest fled. Egwene then says they need to finally siege the White Tower. Turns out there were some of the Heads of Ajahs from the White Tower that showed up at the opposite end of a bridge and told Egwene that they have decided that Egwene shall be raised as Amyrlin of the White Tower.

Egwene is raised as Amyrlin of the White Tower, the rebel Aes Sedai are brought into the Tower, and Silviana (the Mistress of Novices that beat Egwene) is named Keeper of the Chronicals for Egwene. I loved this storyline.


Elaida is taken by the Seanchan and has been collared.


Rand oh yeah, whoa Rand had quite the storyline here. Rand is bringing together some of the Great Captains including Rodel Ituralde now, and Rand has tasked Ituralde to protect the Borderlands. Rand is gearing up for the Last Battle, and he is steamrolling his way to the Last Battle. This book brings Rand to breaking his feeling towards killing women, to also using balefire to kill Forsaken, to nearly killing Tam. A LOT HAPPENED FOR RAND THIS BOOK.

Rand gets captured with the male a'dam by Semirhage, and Semirhage forces Rand to strangle Min. Then Rand feels that this can't be the end, and he feels for more power and finds... the True Power - the Power directly from the Dark One... WHAT?! Then weaves balefire using the True Power and deletes Semirhage and Elza from the Pattern. Previously, in this book Rand woke up in the TAR and spoke with Moridin (who is actually ISHAMAEL! I WAS RIGHT!), and realized that he was able to come back and decided that balefire is what is needed to kill the Forsaken so they won't come back. I have a Forsaken count later, it's important now that we are so close to the Last Battle!

Later Rand finds out where Graendal is located, a palace known as Natrin's Barrow. Rand proceedes to balefire the ENTIRE PALACE, including every single person - servant - cook - janitor.. everyone inside. Two Forsaken killed by balefire this book, but also countless others. Rand got really dark this book.

Rand later, after Cadsuane and Nynaeve brought Tam al'Thor to Rand, nearly kills Tam because Tam let slip that he was brought by Cadsuane; Rand weaved balefire to kill Tam then stopped himself. Then went on a mission to balefire the Tarasian Palace where Fortuona was staying.. it got dark. Then Rand ended up on Dragonmount in one of the most beautifully written chapters I've read.. Rand prepares to die, on Dragonmount like he (LTT) did 3,000 years ago, then realized the cycle of Rebirth means he has a chance to fix his mistakes. Rand uses the Choedan Kal to destroy the Choedan Kal instead of using it to destroy the world. And he laughs...


The Dark One is reaching out of the Pattern and ruining food and moving around hallways of places. We're building to the Last Battle.


The Forsaken some things occured during this book and there needs to be an updated Fosaken Count.. Forsaken count: Forsaken killed by balefire this book: 2.
Alive
Damandred
Mesaana
Lanfear / Cyndane
Ishamael / Moridin
Aginor / Halima / Aran'gar
Moghedien

Balefired
Rahvin
Semirhage
Graendal
Balthamel / Dashiva / Osan'gar
Be'lal

Dead
Asmodeon
Sammael (I THINK!)


The Black Ajah are still out there. A lot were able to escape from the White Tower after Egwene was raised Amyrlin, so I'm interested in finding out more of that. Also, this is also interesting about the Black Ajah Oaths.. "they serve the DO until the hour before their death".. I wonder if that is important.

Title: Knife of Dreams (The Wheel of Time, #11)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Written Review

Robert Jordan, an absolutely beautiful installment in the Wheel of Time and your final Wheel of Time book prior to your untimely passing. This was a spectacular book, specifically in the series, but also a book. There is action, intrigue, resolutions, spontaneity, and so much more happening. After multiple books of slow progress we get a book of resolutions! This was just amazing! More to come in the spoiler review.



Spoiler Review

Oh what a victory lap from Robert Jordan with this book. An absolutly amazing book in this series. SO MANY RESOLUTIONS IN THIS BOOK, let's go through it all! And, most of this will be more from the Story Breakdown...



Thom reveals to Mat that Moiraine is alive and being held captive by the Aelfinn and Eelfinn. They formulate a plan to infiltrate the Aelfinn and Eelfin using the Tower of Ghenjei thanks to Olver’s knowledge of the game of Snakes and Foxes. Mat and Noal agree to accompany Thom on his mission to save Moiraine. Perrin finally saves Faile.. FINALLY! She has been taken for three books now, and wow, finally we got the saving. Elayne is now Queen of Andor! Mat reunites with Talmanes and an even larger and better equipped Band of the Red Hand. Together they launch a campaign of guerilla warfare against the Seanchan army sent by Suroth to murder Tuon. They are found by Furyk Karede, who takes over protection of Tuon and gives Mat intelligence to destroy the Seanchan force. Before returning and assuming power in Ebou Dar, Tuon completes the marriage ceremony with Mat, making him the Prince of Ravens. Rand goes to meet with the Daughter of the Nine Moons, though he discovers it is Semirhage in disguise. Rand loses his hand in the confrontation, burned away by the Forsaken’s attack while Lews Therin battled him for control of saidin. Semirhage is taken captive. Both the rebel Aes Sedai and Red Sisters from the Tower send representatives to the Black Tower to bond Asha’man as Warders. Taim agrees and says, “Let the Lord of Chaos rule,” a phrase previously used by the Forsaken. WHAT COULD THAT MEAN?!

Title: Crossroads of Twilight (The Wheel of Time, #10)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review: Oh wow, this book was hard to read. Boring and slow, and nothing really happened. The worst Wheel of Time book so far. I wanted this book to be so interesting and it really wasn't. The ending of book nine Winter's Heart, this had a lot to live up to and it fell very flat. Nothing really happened and that's why I struggled with this book. So many chapters were too long, and too boring, and nothing really projected to plot. I'm super excited to get to the next book. Wow.

Title: The Path of Daggers (The Wheel of Time, #8)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Oh wow did this book surpass A Crown of Swords, oh wow! I struggled with A Crown of Swords and this was, by no means, a struggle. SO GOOD! We got some resolution to something’s that have been devastating our characters for books now, and some characters are going off the deep end. There was action, intrigue, and an ending that is leading to more and more!

There are questions I have and I’m excited to get reading onto the next book, I have the privilege of owning all the books. Luckily this series has been completed since 2013 so that’s awesome! I’ve thoroughly enjoyed the series so far.

This book brought back the joy I had with the Wheel of Time. As I said, I struggled with the last book and this was a good book.

Title: A Crown of Swords (The Wheel of Time, #7)
Author: Robert Jordan

Title: Lord of Chaos (The Wheel of Time #6)
Author: Robert Jordan

Title: The Fires of Heaven (The Wheel of Time #5)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
This was an amazing Wheel of Time Book! The stakes are raised higher and higher! This book was one of my favorite so far. Each book gets better and better, really. This world is blowing up (getting bigger) and I'm constantly impressed by Robert Jordan and his writting! WOW. SUCH A GOOD BOOK!

Thoughts on FOH
Good grief. This was an awesome book. I'll need to reread my notes and think about my thoughts for each book to decide, but this was an EPIC ending. Glory? Yet to be seen.

Well, Rand, LTT, Rand is AWESOME. I am so excited to read more about Rand. I want to learn so much more about the Power and everything. THIS IS GREAT! Aviendha

Notes as I read:

Well, I'll have to say I am pleasanly surprised by this book!

A Scandal in Bohemia

 Great!

The Red-Headed League

The Case of Identity

I'm Started With Book Three

Title: The Fellowship of the Ring (The Lord of the Rings, #1)
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
My Review:
Written Review

GOOD GRIEF! This was absolutely amazing. I am impressed. I've always loved the Lord of the Rings movies, of course who doesn't?! But I'd never read the books, and I was expecting an absolutely perfect book with no issues at all here. The hype was VERY real with this book and the whole series for that matter. So, that was going to be difficult to reach... but, DID IT EVER REACH THAT HYPE! WOW WOW WOW!

Review Score

Dear Lord, this was so so so good! There was a chapter in this book that was, technically, an info dump. And geez, was it PERFECT! I was not bored by reading it, and I was smiling ear to ear the entire time! That doesn't discount the beginning of this book, because the first 150 pages took a lot out of me, I nearly stopped reading.. I talked to a few people on a few discord servers and I kept on. And wow, am I happy that I kept on!

Total score: 4.6/5 and we round up 'round here! 5 out of 5!

Emotional Impact 5
I smiled through most of this book. My mind was blown multiple times. My heart raced at places. I also wanted to stop reading this book. But... the last two-thirds of the book made up for the beginning for me! WOO!
Characters 5
Love these characters. The names were a bit confusing in the beginning, but as we got out of the Shire, that seemed to resolve.
Plot 4
AMAZING Plot. I loved it! As I keep saying, I struggled in the first third of this book so.. 4.
Dialogue / Prose 4
I struggled with the beginning of the book, and it picked up, but the prose seemed to be lacking a little for me. I have favorites like Brandon Sanderson and Robert Jordan. Those are both different from each other, but I wanted a little more from J.R.R. Tolkien in this book. I was confused a lot with the descriptions of a lot of locations and the dialogue has a lot of singing.. you may like that, but I am not a huge fan of that.
Cinematography / World Building 5
I cannot even begin to explain how amazing this book is - specifically for World Building. I am writing this with a smile that is hurting my cheeks. SO SO GOOD!

Title: The Martian
Author: Andy Weir
My Review:
The Martian Book Review
By Andy Weir

Written Review
A somewhat small scale science fiction story about an astronaut stranded on Mars, and his fight to get back to earth. An interesting premise for sure. And this book also has a lot of critical acclaim too, there is even a movie staring Mat Damon! But sadly, this book fell short for me. I struggled with this book.

I finished in 5 days, and I did not enjoy my time. There were moments of fun and excitement, but by the end I really wanted to be done. There is a lot of science in this book, and I mean chemistry and physics, and so much more.

It is pretty daunting at first, and it doesn’t get much better throughout - but that is to be expected in a science fiction story about an astronaut stranded on Mars; you’ll want the story to be believable. And that’s one thing I’ll get this book credit for, this is completely believable - to my lay mind of course, but this is not a fantasy story by any stretch, there is no magic to be found and the characters are all humans and very believable.

Review Score
I apologize to Andy Weir and his fans, but I’m going to score this book a 3/5. I did not enjoy my time with this book. I’m worried that this might be a trend for me, regarding science fiction, but I’ve got some more sci-fi to read so we shall see.

Also
If you do decide to read this book, I’d like you to count how many people grab their chin while thinking… it’s more than you think.

Project Hail Mary
Will I give Andy Weir’s next book a try… maybe. Only time will tell.

Title: The Terminal List
Author: Jack Carr
My Review:
The Terminal List by Jack Carr
Book Review

Written Review
An absolutely blood pumping military thriller, with completely disgusting murders, with an incredibly intriguing story with twists and turns everywhere. From start to finish, I had no idea what was going to happen. I started off with a low score of a 3/5 because the book was just “okay” and then, some scenes took me to a 5/5. Then some gruesome descriptions of water-boarding and literal gutting of a person comes up, and that didn’t drop the score, it was just hard to read - obviously. But what really planted the 4/5 score for me, was the ending. I feel like it was unearned.

James Reece
And truth be told, if you stop and think about these scenarios the main character is put in - there is really no losing. James Reece goes through A LOT in this book. But when it’s his turn for revenge, he legitimately never fails. Never fails at anything and is an expert at nearly everything he does with no backup plan - if he were to fail.

Plot Armor
Now maybe that’s by design, but I read this book not thinking about that, until I finished the book. So, as you read, try not to think about any plot holes or not even plot holes - PLOT ARMOR for James Reece. I’ve heard that term before but it totally applies here.

James Reece can’t die and won’t. He is the main character and the story would fall apart without James. So, James doesn’t get caught by police, ever. James can kill anyone he meets. James can do no wrong.

Score
Rant over, 4/5. A good book, thrilling and violent for sure. But the plot armor is strong with this book.

Title: The Hunger Games (The Hunger Games, #1)
Author: Suzanne Collins
My Review:
Written Review
I'm overall not impressed here. This book was really just fine. I read and listened to this book as I read - which I like to do. It helped me read the book in a totally of 2 days, which means I'm excited to get to read another book now!

Writing Style
I really didn't like the writing style of the book, it could be that I just finished The Dragon Reborn by Robert Jordan with multiple perspectives and also written in third person. This book was written all in first person.. "I crawled under the fence..." and there was also a strange phrasing that said "'how's the meat,' I say". Not I said. It's really kind of hard to explain, I guess it was just written like that so that you really felt like you were Katniss. It was just a little weird for me. I wasn't a big fan of it.

Story
The story was fine, really, it had some twists and turns that were completely out of left field and didn't feel very like the character would do that - maybe I'm looking too much into it, but I wasn't grabbed by the story. It was supposed to be visceral, and maybe because it was a middle grade book it couldn't be super violent. I may have just wanted it to be really violent - that might've made it better for me. I also would've liked multiple perspectives, but that is probably just personal preference again.

Review Score
4/5. I will, more than likely, not be rereading this book. And that's kind of where my score comes from. I didn't find the story fun or very exciting. I just wanted more.

Title: The Dragon Reborn (The Wheel of Time, #3)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Dragon Reborn Review..
Jan 16 23

“I am the hunter now.”

Okay, Let's talk about The Dragon Reborn. First off, this a great book, 5/5. Again another incredible book with a superb story by Robert Jordan in the Wheel of Time. Robert Jordan just knows how to write. He knows how to build a world and paint a picture of exactly what is happening and how everything and everyone looks. I'm constantly impressed by the design of the world and the characters and the settings of literally everywhere. This book takes some time to start up, because we have a bit of a recap at the beginning and we have to get caught up on where all the characters are - but you'll enjoy it for sure.

Spoilers
I have some new favorite characters now..

Mat and Perrin
These boys have become more favorites of mine... their story has come to this point - now we have learned a lot more about their powers and we are just getting a little bit of Mat's power, but the more I read Mat's perspective.. and yes - we get some Mat perspectives, I love it so much! Mat is funny and quippy and that's amazing. He's also really good with his Quaterstaff and I like that - he is becoming a great character for me.

Perrin is an amazing person. A great character to read and feel for. He is kind and sweet and smart and slowly becoming my favorite character. The way he cares for people is second to none, he looks out for all. He is quiet and is just great - I love it!

The Hunters Three
These girls.. Elayne, Egwene, and Nynaeve have got it going on! They are leveling up like no other - and I'll talk about that some more when I get to it, but wow. The way they are so absolutely incredibly powerful is just a testament to their characters - such great parts of the story and quite possibly some of the most important. I love them! So funny as well, when it's important.

Loial
I can't say enough, he's just great. Also so kind and caring and good when it counts.I love that we got to see more of him interacting with the Ogier - so good to read.

Rand
The Dragon Reborn, "I am Rand al'Thor.. I am the Dragon Reborn." ABSOLUTELY AMAZING STUFF. We didn't see much of Rand this book, and that was a bit disappointing, but what we got of Rand was powerful. I loved reading his scenes, even those scenes where he absolutely murdered a dog and a group of people - because he is GOING MAD. I hope! And finally we got Callandor and it is even better than I expected. I couldn't believe it could've been cooler than I thought, but the power is immense and I love reading about it. I love it.

Review Scrore
Without a doubt 5/5.

What's Next to Read
As you might've read.. in (tbr 2023), I cannot buy anymore books until I read all the books on my shelf.. so I'm thinking I might jump to The Hunger Games. That sounds like fun, I started it early in 2022, but I was reading multiple books at the time and have since been only reading one book at a time.. here's to turning another page! Oooo, I like that as a catch phrase ha

Title: Warbreaker
Author: Brandon Sanderson
My Review:
WARBREAKER REVIEW
OCTOBER 27, 2022
Warbreaker; A story of politics, dead things coming back to life, a magic system built on keeping or bringing things back to life, colors to the most impeccable distinction, a great story of a sister coming to save her sister from what she thinks is pure evil, and also a little bit of a sexual component.

SPOILER ALERT

Written Review
** spoiler alert **

Warbreaker; A story of politics, dead things coming back to life, a magic system built on keeping or bringing things back to life, colors to the most impeccable distinction, a great story of a sister coming to save her sister from what she thinks is pure evil, and also a little bit of a sexual component.

This story is great, these characters are great, this world is great, Brandon Sanderson is great. I’m continually impressed as I read these Brandon Sanderson books at the complexity of these magic systems and worlds. He truly does some amazing work, realizing what could and should happens on these planets, and how everything connects.

Also, I really appreciate this book and how it was released. It was released on Brandon’s website first, then eventually completed and published. But you can read the entire book for free on his site. Also, there are annotations for this book on the site as well, so you get Brandon’s thoughts right as you read the book too.

Forms
I chose to read this book in a few forms, as I typically do with Brandon books. They’re some long books, and I like to read on my lunch breaks at work, where other coworkers are conversing. So I find it fun to listen to the audiobook while I read the book in front of me. Really gives me that tactile feel of the book, which the incredible voice acting behind the audiobooks. For Warbreaker, being that the book was on Brandon’s site, I chose to use the Kindle copy of the book! This was my first real experience with a kindle and it was a great one! I’m using the Kindle 10th generation; buy that Kindle here!

Kindle Impressions

I actually filmed my first impressions of this kindle just days after a new kindle was released, and at the time of writing, the impressions video has over 1,000 views! That’s crazy for my channel.

But reading on the kindle has been an enjoyable experience. I won’t say it’s a perfect experience, but it’s enjoyable. I don’t really want to read things on my phone, if I’m reading a book. But the kindle is nice because it is really pretty distraction-free reading and it’s a good size and weight.

The Spoils
I thought this book was incredible. I was shocked when certain characters died, like Lightsong. I was shocked when certain characters didn't die, like Vasher. I was open mouth shocked when Blushweaver was killed. I had no idea that was even possible. I'm so excited to learn more about this world, there might be a sequel, but that is not planned or set to release anytime soon.

I honestly found this book to be just the right amount of sexual. Strange phrasing I know, but there were moments when this story had to talk about sex. The crux of the story was - the Idrian's were to give the GodKing an heir. Interestingly the GodKing didn't know how to do that. Eventually coitus is completed. It's never vulgar or on-screen. But sex is important in this book, and I didn't expect that.

Vasher
Vasher is awesome. I apologize to spoil some of the Stormlight Archive but, Vasher is Zahel.. I know right?! It's so clear after reading this novel. Want to be more spoiled, here is the Coppermind Wiki for Vasher.

Vasher is great in this book. There are so many twists in this book, I wasn't sure if Vasher was a good guy or bad. In fact, I still don't know. Maybe he is just Chaotic Good, like Hoid? But Hoid also isn't always good, so I don't really know.

Nightblood
I love comedy, and I love comedic relief. I didn't realize that could come from a talking sword that wants to destroy evil. When I say talking sword, I don't mean audibly, just in thoughts of those Nightblood thinks should hear.

Spoilers for Words of Radiance, NIGHTBLOOD! Szeth has Nightblood. That means Nightblood is close to Vasher, on Roshar.

Vivenna
Vivenna is awesome. I really enjoy this aspect of the Royal Locks, that was an interesting part of the magic system. I'm also very proud of Vivenna for staying alive. She is very powerful.

Lightsong
Lightsong is awesome. Lightsong might be my favorite part of this novel. Another comedic relief, but also he cares about the people he loves. He fanes comedy but really feels a lot. He worries a lot about if he is really who people say he is, a god.

Besides the god thing, I align very closely to how Lightsong feels. So I really like Lightsong.

Score
Warbreaker by Brandon Sanderson 5/5.

What's Next?
If you've read What am I Reading Next?, then you'd know that Edgedancer by Brandon Sanderson is next! I'm really on a Brandon Sanderson kick right now, and I'm going to try and read a lot of Brandon Sanderson back to back for the forseeable future.

I highly recommend this book, and I imagine I will review all Brandon's book like that... we shall see.

Title: The Great Hunt (The Wheel of Time #2)
Author: Robert Jordan
My Review:
Written Review
Oh wow, eee, wow! This book is amazing. SO many things happen in this book that make this book great. This is a great sequel to The Eye of the World, at that. I've been with these characters for so long already - 1400 pages now! And so much has already happened to all these characters, I care about them, I want to see what happens next, I want to see what changes with these characters of 15 books. I am also a big fan of the way Robert Jordan writes. He is very descriptive, and a lot of reviewers say that RJ is too descriptive, but I love it - now, I've not read all 15 books but I'm really loving it right now. Mat has become my new favorite character, I didn't really like him at first, I felt he were a bit of an annoying character with nothing to say - but now, I cannot wait to see what happens with Mat. Hopefully he can be saved. I can't wait to read book three - The Dragon Reborn!

Spoiler
Okay, let's spoil!

Thom
THOM! Thom is alive, and is even more awesome than he was in the first book - I love the leveling up that the Two River folk and how we get to talk to someone who we've not seen in a long time. I love that the character that we get to talk to is Thom, I love Thom! I am almost certain we are going to see more of Thom… hopefully.

My Love of Fantasy
I do really love that the Two River folk are leveling up and getting better at things and learning new things. Obviously that would happen, but that's why I love reading fantasy and it makes me so happy to read things like this - stories of characters who get better and we follow them for a LONG TIME.

Sword Forms
I really enjoy reading the way RJ writes his fight scenes, with the Sword Forms without much else - it is great..

THE HORN
I could not believe that the horn would be blown in this book - because the prophecy says the Horn is tied to who blows it and it must be at the Last Battle. And now, apparently it's tied to Mat? I have no idea - I guess I MUST READ AND FIND OUT!

The Dragon
Here it is.. Rand is the Dragon Reborn and I can't wait to see more of him pronouncing himself as the Dragon Reborn. I cried here - when Artur Hawkwing asked if Lews Theron had his banner.. Oh man I'm about to cry right now. THIS IS SO AWESOME. Wow, what a story.

Heron Marked Sword
Okay, let's talk about the sword. I don't know much about the sword on the image of the Dragon Reborn cover but I'm guessing it's going to be Rand's new sword? It's all white. But, the Heron Marked Sword was such a big deal for the first two books and now Rand (DRAGON REBORN) has TWO HERONS on his body. Oh wow, this story is amazing….

Ingtar
Oh man.. A Darkfriend. I couldn't believe this. I knew something was up with him the way he talked about the Horn. I had no idea this was going to happen and it hurt so much - but I feel his death was awesome. Hurin and everyone wanted to save him. So sad.

Review Score
Without a doubt a 5/5. Amazing. The ending really was incredible. I couldn't believe this book could get better and then the ending RAMPED UP!

That was an infuriating mess of a book. 2/5. The more I read, the more I didn’t want to read. I don’t understand this book. And maybe that’s my fault, maybe I “just don’t get it”. But, by golly, this book was bad. Luckily I finished this in 4 days, but that’s because I skipped about 40 pages, jumping through paragraphs, seeing if anything caught my attention. But no. I could go on, but I am onto the next book.

Notes from my readings:

Oh man, this is hard to read. Like it’s taking a lot out of me to read this book. I really don’t want to stop reading this, to read another book, because I might not want to read this book when I come back to it.

So, I’m going to continue to try to read 1984, and we’ll see what happens.

93. Hmm, at first I felt I was going to have to shelve this. But, reading more I found much more interest. I’m excited to keep reading.

148. Into Part Two now. We’ve met Julia and O’Brien has just invited us to his home. I feel as though this book could’ve been like 40 pages maybe? I only say that because of the author’s, George Orwell, need for such clarification. He seems to harp, or spend a lot of time in great detail on each particular subject. Like page after page of description and detail and feeling, when the plot is only a paragraph or two. It’s taken nearly half the book to get to an exciting point. And maybe that’s my own fault for wanting this to be faster, maybe that’s Orwell’s writing style. Maybe I’m just wanting a tighter experience from my Orwellian Dystopian Past-Future story.

200. I’m infuriated. Maybe that’s not the right word. I just wish I would love this book. I’m finding it hard to love this book. As mentioned, Orwell is doubleplus detailed, to use some Newspeak. Page after page, on and on, again and again, over and over, everything goes; with little to know “meat”. No substance of any kind on most pages. I want to love this book. I’m about to start Part Three, and I feel as though this book could’ve been about half this size. Now I have 79 pages left, and we are at a bit of an exciting cliffhanger. And that’s not what I’ve been known to expect with this book, so far. Let’s see how this goes! I’m at around a 3/5 so far.

Title: Aftermath (Star Wars: Aftermath, #1)
Author: Chuck Wendig
My Review:
An engaging story of the New Republic and the Empire! The beginning, of course, is a slow build, meeting new characters with storied histories. Then, a metaphoric flip is switched and we are off to the races.

The second half of the book is a whirlwind of emotions, of triumphs, of defeats, of cliff hangers, all to a satisfying ending.

Where to next? Luckily there are two more books in this series!

Chuck Wendig, nice job!

P.S. I just "got back into" reading this year, and I started off with this book, and this book revitalized my love of reading. I have quite a large TBR(to be read) now, and that's some thanks to this book in particular.

P.P.S. If you're a Star Wars fan, this is a MUST READ!
